ori.io.description: Standard Input/Output utilities. Provides functions to read from and write to the terminal console.

ori.fs.description: File system access and management module. Provides synchronous and asynchronous options to check, read, write, and manipulate files and directories on the local machine.

ori.bytes.description: Raw byte buffers utilities. Allows operations such as hex conversions, slice manipulation, and list conversions.

ori.string.description: String utility operations. Provides length checking, splitting, search filters, case modifications, and conversions to raw byte arrays.

ori.math.description: General mathematical constants and helper routines. Contains basic arithmetic bounds checking, rounding, trigonometry, and logarithms.

ori.net.description: Networking module. Provides interfaces for launching TCP/UDP servers and establishing network connection streams.

ori.time.description: Time measurement, clock manipulation, and thread execution suspension routines.

ori.json.description: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) parser and encoder. Allows serializing Ori values into strings and deserializing JSON payloads back to language structures.

ori.random.description: Pseudorandom number generation (PRNG) routines for generating floats and integers within ranges.

ori.path.description: File system path parser. Allows joining segments, resolving absolute paths, and extracting filenames or extensions.

ori.list.description: List manipulation core module. Houses size checks, indexing operations, pushing, popping, and slice manipulations.

ori.map.description: Hash map (key-value dictionary) utilities for storing, retrieving, and checking keys.

ori.set.description: Unique set collection helpers for set arithmetic, values membership tests, inserts, and removes.

ori.io.print.description: Writes a string to the standard output without appending a newline.
ori.io.print.applicability: Used for printing prompt messages, building progress indicators, or custom formatted output.
ori.io.print.example:
import ori.io as io

func main()
    io.print("Enter value: ")
end

ori.io.println.description: Writes a string to the standard output, followed by a newline.
ori.io.println.applicability: Used for printing log messages, general command line outputs, and standard debugging text.
ori.io.println.example:
import ori.io as io

func main()
    io.println("Application started successfully.")
end

ori.fs.read_file.description: Reads the entire contents of a file into a bytes buffer asynchronously/synchronously depending on execution context.
ori.fs.read_file.applicability: Essential for processing binary files, loading text resources, or parsing local configuration files.
ori.fs.read_file.example:
import ori.fs as fs
import ori.io as io

func main()
    const res = fs.read_file("config.json")
    match res
        success(data) => io.println("Loaded data: " + string(data))
        error(err) => io.println("Failed to read config.")
    end
end

ori.fs.write_file.description: Writes a bytes buffer into a file, creating it if it does not exist, or overwriting it if it does.
ori.fs.write_file.applicability: Used for saving application state, writing logs, creating temporary storage, or exporting reports.
ori.fs.write_file.example:
import ori.fs as fs
import ori.io as io

func main()
    const payload = bytes.from_list([72, 101, 108, 108, 111]) // "Hello"
    const res = fs.write_file("output.txt", payload)
    match res
        success(void) => io.println("Write complete.")
        error(err) => io.println("Write failed.")
    end
end

ori.bytes.to_hex.description: Converts a raw bytes buffer into its hexadecimal string representation.
ori.bytes.to_hex.applicability: Widely used in cryptographic hashing visualizations, generating checksum strings, and formatting binary protocol payloads for debugging.

ori.bytes.from_hex.description: Parses a hexadecimal string and returns the corresponding raw bytes buffer.
ori.bytes.from_hex.applicability: Useful for decoding hex-encoded files, processing public-key certificates, or parsing network packets represented as hex.
ori.bytes.from_hex.example:
import ori.bytes as bytes
import ori.io as io

func main()
    const hex_str = "48656c6c6f"
    const data = bytes.from_hex(hex_str)
    io.println(string(data)) // Prints "Hello"
end

ori.math.utils.clamp_int.description: Restricts an integer value to be within a specified minimum and maximum range.
ori.math.utils.clamp_int.applicability: Commonly used in game logic (constraining characters within screen boundaries), UI layouts (clamping coordinate systems), and index math.

ori.math.utils.lerp.description: Performs linear interpolation between two float values based on a weight ratio.
ori.math.utils.lerp.applicability: Essential for graphics development, game engines (smooth translation/transitions), and easing animations.

ori.string.utils.contains.description: Checks if a string contains a specific substring.
ori.string.utils.contains.applicability: Used in text processing, simple search filters, and matching query parameters.

ori.random.int_range.description: Generates a pseudorandom integer between the specified min and max bounds (inclusive of min, exclusive of max).
ori.random.int_range.applicability: Commonly used in gaming (rolling dice, picking indexes), generating unique IDs, and random selection algorithms.

ori.random.float.description: Generates a pseudorandom floating-point number between 0.0 and 1.0.
ori.random.float.applicability: Useful for generating percentages, probability weights, random noise multipliers, and easing variations.

ori.time.sleep.description: Suspends execution of the current thread/task for a given duration in milliseconds.
ori.time.sleep.applicability: Used in loops to set intervals, rate-limiting requests, implementing game cycles, or delays in automation.
ori.time.sleep.example:
import ori.time as time
import ori.io as io

func main()
    io.println("Waiting...")
    time.sleep(1000) // Delay 1 second
    io.println("Finished.")
end

ori.time.now.description: Returns the current system timestamp in UNIX epoch seconds or milliseconds.
ori.time.now.applicability: Used for benchmarks, logging date metrics, calculating task durations, and generating unique ID seeds.

ori.json.encode.description: Serializes supported Ori data structures (like maps, lists, and structs) into a JSON formatted string.
ori.json.encode.applicability: Used for exporting application configs, communicating with REST APIs, and storing structured logs.

ori.json.decode.description: Deserializes a JSON string payload back into dynamic Ori objects or types.
ori.json.decode.applicability: Required for parsing API responses, loading configuration files, or handling JSON log structures.
ori.json.decode.example:
import ori.json as json
import ori.io as io

func main()
    const payload = "{\"active\":true}"
    const config = json.decode(payload)
    match config
        success(obj) => io.println("Decoded successfully")
        error(err) => io.println("Failed to decode JSON")
    end
end

ori.path.join.description: Combines list segments into a unified file system path, automatically resolving separator slashes.
ori.path.join.applicability: Used to dynamically build local paths cross-platform without worrying about '/' vs '\' differences.

ori.string.split.description: Splits a string by a delimiter substring, returning a list of segments.
ori.string.split.applicability: Useful for CSV processing, parsing command arguments, and text segment extraction.
